{"body":"**Uberon term**
cuticle development
regulation of epidermal cell differentiation
stem cell differentiation

and others....

**Bug description**

this is prompted by a (now very old)  ticket in the GO where many plant annotations are being flagged as taxon constraint violations because of associations to UBERON terms. Pascale Guadet requested we open a ticket her in Uberon.

https://github.com/geneontology/go-ontology/issues/30555

Reviewing our latest snapshot for GO rule violations, we noticed a bunch of new taxon violations for old Arabidopsis annotations to these terms. (sample only, there are more)

cuticle development
regulation of epidermal cell differentiation
stem cell differentiation

Was there a mass mapping of some older GO terms to UBERON terms for logical defs and the GO terms then acquired their taxon constraints? Can we revisit some of these and address the taxon constraint issues?","comments":[{"id":"IC_kwDOAAidd88AAAABAVsQDw","author":{"login":"cmungall"},"authorAssociation":"MEMBER","body":"@dragon-ai-agent please analyze the use of the term cuticle across metazoa, suggest more appropriate specific classes for different clades.

this is what we have now:

```yaml
id: UBERON:0001002
name: cuticle
def: "Tough but flexible, non-mineral outer coverings of an organism, or parts of an organism, that provide protection. Cuticles are non-homologous, differing in their origin, structure and chemical composition[WP]." [http://sourceforge.net/tracker/?func=detail&aid=3348965&group_id=36855&atid=440764, Wikipedia:Cuticle]
comment: EDITOR_NOTE TODO - check cuticle vs exoskeleton. Note that GO has a very generic definition of cuticle. See also arthropod-anatomy ontology. Note in ncit Cuticle is a plant part
subset: grouping_class
subset: non_informative
subset: uberon_slim
synonym: "cuticula" RELATED [BTO:0001600]
synonym: "cuticular exoskeleton" RELATED []
xref: BTO:0001600
xref: Wikipedia:Cuticle
is_a: UBERON:0000476 ! acellular anatomical structure
disjoint_from: UBERON:0004882 ! eponychium
relationship: in_taxon NCBITaxon:6072 {source="Rieger - Evolution of the Cuticle in the Lower Eumetazoa"} ! Eumetazoa
```","createdAt":"2026-04-25T03:04:30Z","includesCreatedEdit":false,"isMinimized":false,"minimizedReason":"","reactionGroups":[{"content":"EYES","users":{"totalCount":1}}],"url":"https://github.com/obophenotype/uberon/issues/3694#issuecomment-4317712399","viewerDidAuthor":false},{"id":"IC_kwDOAAidd88AAAABAVsXWw","author":{"login":"ai4c-agent"},"authorAssociation":"NONE","body":"🤖 Working on it...

Follow along: [View workflow run](https://github.com/obophenotype/uberon/actions/runs/24921090237)

subsetdef: common_anatomy "Terms applicable across life, not restricted to Metazoa"

subsetdef: inconsistent_with_fma "classes that have some inconsistency with FMA"
subsetdef: location_grouping ""
subsetdef: major_organ "somewhat fuzzy grouping for analysis purposes, currently composed of something like: liver, heart, skeletal, kidney, bladder, brain, skin, mouth, esophagus, stomach, small intestine, large intestines, trachea nose, lungs, brain, spinal cord,  peripheral nerves, kidneys, ureters, bladder, urethra, gonads"

[Term]
id: UBERON:0001002
name: cuticle
def: "Tough but flexible, non-mineral outer coverings of an organism, or parts of an organism, that provide protection. Cuticles are non-homologous, differing in their origin, structure and chemical composition[WP]." [http://sourceforge.net/tracker/?func=detail&aid=3348965&group_id=36855&atid=440764, Wikipedia:Cuticle]
comment: EDITOR_NOTE TODO - check cuticle vs exoskeleton. Note that GO has a very generic definition of cuticle. See also arthropod-anatomy ontology. Note in ncit Cuticle is a plant part
subset: grouping_class
subset: non_informative
subset: uberon_slim
synonym: "cuticula" RELATED [BTO:0001600]
synonym: "cuticular exoskeleton" RELATED []
xref: BTO:0001600
xref: Wikipedia:Cuticle
is_a: UBERON:0000476 ! acellular anatomical structure
disjoint_from: UBERON:0004882 ! eponychium
relationship: in_taxon NCBITaxon:6072 {source="Rieger - Evolution of the Cuticle in the Lower Eumetazoa"} ! Eumetazoa
property_value: has_relational_adjective "cuticular" xsd:string

subsetdef: added_by_HRA "Classes tagged with this subset property were added on request from HuBMAP to support the HuBMAP Human Reference Atlas (HRA)."
subsetdef: added_for_HCA "Classes tagged with this subset property were added upon request from the Human Cell Atlas (HCA)."
subsetdef: common_anatomy "Terms applicable across life, not restricted to Metazoa"
subsetdef: cumbo "CUMBO"
subsetdef: cyclostome_subset "cyclostome structures"
subsetdef: defined_by_cytoarchitecture "brain region defined by cyto-architecture"
subsetdef: defined_by_ordinal_series "classes that are defined by relative position counting from first in a series of elements along an axis in an individual organism rather than by strict homology"
subsetdef: developmental_classification "developmental_classification"
subsetdef: dubious_grouping "Indicates that subclasses in AOs may be inappropriately linked"
subsetdef: early_development "A class that represents an early developmental structure, like a blastocyst. This part of the ontology is undergoing review to remove inappropriate grouping classes."
subsetdef: efo_slim "EFO slim"
subsetdef: emapa_ehdaa2 "derived from the union of EHDAA2 and EMAPA - still to be checked"
subsetdef: feed_aligned "subset of classes that have logical defs text defs and synonyms aligned with FEED"
subsetdef: functional_classification "functional_classification"
subsetdef: grouping_class "Subset consisting of classes creating for grouping purposes"
subsetdef: homology_grouping "A grouping class that depends on an assumption of homology between subclasses"
subsetdef: human_reference_atlas ""
subsetdef: inconsistent_with_fma "classes that have some inconsistency with FMA"
subsetdef: location_grouping ""
subsetdef: major_organ "somewhat fuzzy grouping for analysis purposes, currently composed of something like: liver, heart, skeletal, kidney, bladder, brain, skin, mouth, esophagus, stomach, small intestine, large intestines, trachea nose, lungs, brain, spinal cord,  peripheral nerves, kidneys, ureters, bladder, urethra, gonads"
subsetdef: metazoa_core "core classes typically found across metazoa. one purpose is to create a rough set of terms that could be used to start a new metazoan AO. Note this subset is not yet fully fleshed out"
subsetdef: non_informative "abstract class brought in to group ontology classes but not informative"
subsetdef: organ_slim "organs, excluding individual muscles and skeletal elements"
subsetdef: pheno_slim "Phenotype slim"
subsetdef: phenotype_rcn "A subset specifically created for the 2012 Phenotype RCN meeting. Includes some human-specific terms that may eventually be removed when they are adequately represented with part of relationships in FMA"
subsetdef: uberon_slim "Uberon slim - subset that excludes obscure terms and deep compositional terms"
subsetdef: unverified_taxonomic_grouping "Indicates that the class encompasses classes from other AOs that have not been verified"
subsetdef: upper_level "abstract upper-level terms not directly useful for analysis"
subsetdef: vertebrate_core "core classes typically found across vertebrates. one purpose is to create a rough set of terms that could be used to start a new vertebrate AO"

10340	property_value: homology_notes "The continuation of the archinephric duct, now called the deferent duct, extends caudally to the cloaca or to the part of the mammalian urethra that is derived from the cloaca.[well established][VHOG]" xsd:string {date_retrieved="2012-09-17", external_class="VHOG:0001135", ontology="VHOG", source="ISBN:978-0030223693 Liem KF, Bemis WE, Walker WF, Grande L, Functional Anatomy of the Vertebrates: An Evolutionary Perspective (2001) p.670", source="http://bgee.unil.ch/"}
10341	property_value: taxon_notes "Most vertebrates have some form of duct to transfer the sperm from the testes to the urethra. In cartilaginous fish and amphibians, sperm is carried through the archinephric duct, which also partially helps to transport urine from the kidneys. In teleosts, there is a distinct sperm duct, separate from the ureters, and often called the vas deferens, although probably not truly homologous with that in humans. In cartilaginous fishes, the part of the archinephric duct closest to the testis is coiled up to form an epididymis. Below this are a number of small glands secreting components of the seminal fluid. The final portion of the duct also receives ducts from the kidneys in most species. In amniotes, however, the archinephric duct has become a true vas deferens, and is used only for conducting sperm, never urine. As in cartilaginous fish, the upper part of the duct forms the epididymis. In many species, the vas deferens ends in a small sac for storing sperm. The only vertebrates to lack any structure resembling a vas deferens are the primitive jawless fishes, which release sperm directly into the body cavity, and then into the surrounding water through a simple opening in the body wall." xsd:string {source="WP"}

cuticle development
regulation of epidermal cell differentiation
stem cell differentiation

cat > /tmp/issue_3694_comment.md << 'EOF'
# Analysis of UBERON "cuticle" across Metazoa

## 1. Current state in UBERON

| ID | Name | Taxon | Notes |
|---|---|---|---|
| UBERON:0001002 | cuticle | `in_taxon` Eumetazoa (NCBITaxon:6072) | `grouping_class`, `non_informative`, `uberon_slim`; defined as explicitly *non-homologous* |
| UBERON:0001001 | chitin-based cuticle | `in_taxon` Arthropoda (NCBITaxon:6656) | equivalent: `cuticle` and `composed_primarily_of` chitin |
| UBERON:0003049 | collagen and cuticulin-based cuticle | `in_taxon` Nematoda (NCBITaxon:6231) | `taxon_notes`: "currently restricted to nematodes, but this may be too strict" |
| UBERON:0003201 | exocuticle | Arthropoda | Layer of arthropod procuticle |
| UBERON:0003202 | endocuticle | Arthropoda | Layer of arthropod procuticle |
| UBERON:0002076 | cuticle of hair | (implicit vertebrate) | Mammalian hair shaft outer cell layer — a total misnomer semantically, shares only the name |
| UBERON:0006693 | obsolete protein-based cuticle | — | Obsolete, see also GO:0007592 |

## 2. Why the current model causes the GO problem

GO's `cuticle development` (and relatives) have logical definitions that reference `UBERON:0001002 cuticle`. Because UBERON:0001002 carries `in_taxon Eumetazoa`, any GO term that uses it inherits a constraint that forbids plant annotations — producing the Arabidopsis taxon violations reported in [geneontology/go-ontology#30555](https://github.com/geneontology/go-ontology/issues/30555).

Note also that the text definition of UBERON:0001002 explicitly states *"Cuticles are non-homologous, differing in their origin, structure and chemical composition"* — which already acknowledges that the class is a heterogeneous grouping, not a natural kind. The `in_taxon Eumetazoa` is really an editorial choice to delegate plant cuticle to PO, not a biological statement about homology.

## 3. Biological survey: cuticle-bearing metazoan clades

### Ecdysozoa (all defined by periodic molting of a chitinous/proteinaceous cuticle)
- **Arthropoda** — chitin + sclerotized cuticular proteins (resilin, cuticulins). ✅ UBERON:0001001
- **Tardigrada** — chitinous cuticle with sclerified plates; molted. ❌ missing (currently would fall under arthropod cuticle, which is wrong)
- **Onychophora** — thin chitinous cuticle with papillae; molted. ❌ missing
- **Nematoda** — collagen + cuticulin + glycoproteins; molted four times in development. ✅ UBERON:0003049
- **Nematomorpha** — thick chitinous cuticle; molted. ❌ missing
- **Priapulida / Kinorhyncha / Loricifera (Scalidophora)** — chitinous cuticle bearing scalids or lorica; molted. ❌ missing

### Lophotrochozoa (non-molted cuticles; continuously secreted)
- **Annelida** — collagenous cuticle, thin and non-molted. ❌ missing (important — annelid cuticle is collagen-based but structurally distinct from nematode cuticle)
- **Rotifera** — intracytoplasmic lamina: proteinaceous layer *inside* syncytial epidermal cells, not a typical extracellular cuticle. ❌ missing
- **Mollusca** — periostracum, organic conchiolin outer layer of shell; cuticle-like but usually not called "cuticle". Also distinctive cuticle on the foot/mantle in some taxa. ❌ missing
- **Chaetognatha, Entoprocta, Bryozoa** — thin cuticles in various locations. Usually not a priority.
- **Platyhelminthes** — no cuticle; surface is a syncytial tegument (cellular, not acellular).

### Deuterostomia
- **Hemichordata** — delicate cuticle over proboscis and collar in some enteropneusts. Minor.
- **Echinodermata, Chordata** — no true body cuticle.
- **Vertebrata** — the only vertebrate structure bearing the word "cuticle" (UBERON:0002076 cuticle of hair) is a specialized keratinized cell layer on the mammalian hair shaft; it is not homologous or structurally comparable to invertebrate cuticles and should arguably be renamed or at minimum kept logically disjoint from UBERON:0001002.

### Non-bilaterians (Cnidaria, Ctenophora, Porifera, Placozoa)
- No cuticle. The current `in_taxon Eumetazoa` constraint is therefore slightly too broad — a tighter constraint would be `in_taxon Bilateria`.

## 4. Recommendations

### 4a. Re-examine the taxon constraint on UBERON:0001002 (root cause of GO issue)

Three realistic options, from least to most disruptive:

1. **Downgrade to `present_in_taxon` / remove strict `in_taxon`**: UBERON:0001002 is already flagged `grouping_class` and `non_informative`. Because the class is *by construction* non-homologous, a hard `in_taxon` is dubious. Removing it (or replacing with `never_in_taxon` for specific excluded clades like Porifera) lets GO's `cuticle development` apply cross-kingdom without false violations.
2. **Keep Eumetazoa constraint, but fix GO side**: GO should either (a) create cross-kingdom `cuticle development` without the UBERON logical def, or (b) split into clade-specific GO terms that reference the clade-specific UBERON cuticle classes. This pushes the fix to GO.
3. **Tighten to Bilateria** (more correct biologically) and add a parallel plant cuticle grouping via PO cross-reference — doesn't solve the GO problem but is more accurate.

**Recommendation: option 1** — remove `in_taxon Eumetazoa` from UBERON:0001002 and rely on the clade-specific subclasses for taxon constraints. Add a `comment` noting that the class is a non-homologous grouping and plant cuticle is represented in PO.

### 4b. Broaden / correct existing clade-specific classes

- **UBERON:0001001 chitin-based cuticle**: change `in_taxon Arthropoda` → `in_taxon Panarthropoda` (NCBITaxon:88770) to properly include Tardigrada + Onychophora, **or** create a parent `ecdysozoan chitinous cuticle` at the Ecdysozoa level (NCBITaxon:1206794) and put Arthropoda, Tardigrada, Onychophora, Scalidophora, Nematomorpha as sub-taxa of it. The latter is cleaner because it matches the molting character that defines Ecdysozoa.
- **UBERON:0003049 collagen and cuticulin-based cuticle**: keep `in_taxon Nematoda` as is; the taxon_notes caveat can be removed since annelid cuticle is structurally distinct enough to warrant its own class (see below). Consider renaming to `nematode cuticle` for clarity.
- **UBERON:0002076 cuticle of hair**: mark `disjoint_from` UBERON:0001002, or rename (e.g. `hair shaft cuticular layer`) to avoid confusion. It does not belong under the invertebrate cuticle hierarchy.

### 4c. Proposed new clade-specific classes

| Proposed name | Taxon | Logical def / parent | Priority |
|---|---|---|---|
| annelid cuticle | Annelida (NCBITaxon:6340) | `cuticle` and `composed_primarily_of` collagen; **not** molted | High — fills a real gap in Lophotrochozoa |
| tardigrade cuticle | Tardigrada (NCBITaxon:42241) | sub of `chitin-based cuticle` | Medium |
| onychophoran cuticle | Onychophora (NCBITaxon:2074087) | sub of `chitin-based cuticle` | Medium |
| scalidophoran cuticle | Scalidophora (NCBITaxon:2008202) | sub of `chitin-based cuticle` | Low (small clades) |
| nematomorph cuticle | Nematomorpha (NCBITaxon:33310) | sub of `chitin-based cuticle` | Low |
| rotifer cuticle (intracytoplasmic lamina) | Rotifera (NCBITaxon:10190) | sub of `cuticle` with note on intracellular location | Low |
| ecdysozoan cuticle (optional grouping) | Ecdysozoa (NCBITaxon:1206794) | sub of `cuticle`; has_quality `molted` | Medium — captures the molting synapomorphy |

### 4d. Don't forget `chitin-based cuticle development` on the GO side

Note there's already `GO:0040003 chitin-based cuticle development` (taxon-constrained to Arthropoda via UBERON:0001001). If Uberon creates `ecdysozoan cuticle` / `annelid cuticle`, GO can follow with matching `cuticle development` subclasses and the constraint issue evaporates for cuticle-related processes.

## 5. Suggested next step

My recommendation, in order of impact:

1. **Immediate**: Remove `in_taxon Eumetazoa` from UBERON:0001002 (or narrow to `never_in_taxon Porifera/Placozoa/Ctenophora`). This single change fixes the Arabidopsis violations without requiring any GO changes.
2. **Short term**: Add `annelid cuticle` (highest biological value; real lophotrochozoan class currently missing).
3. **Short term**: Broaden UBERON:0001001 to Panarthropoda (or create an `ecdysozoan cuticle` parent).
4. **Optional**: Add tardigrade/onychophoran/scalidophoran/nematomorph cuticle sub-classes as sub-classes of the broadened chitin-based / ecdysozoan cuticle.

Happy to open a PR implementing any subset of these. Please confirm which direction you'd like — in particular, whether to go with option 1 (simplest fix: drop the taxon constraint on UBERON:0001002) or option 2 (GO-side fix) for the root cause, and which new clade-specific classes you want created.

content.text:
I've posted the analysis on the issue. Summary of the recommendation I gave:

**Root-cause fix (one change, resolves the GO taxon violation)**: remove `in_taxon Eumetazoa` from `UBERON:0001002 cuticle`. The text def explicitly says cuticles are non-homologous across clades; the hard taxon constraint propagates via GO logical defs and is what's flagging plant (Arabidopsis) annotations to `cuticle development`.

**Gaps in clade coverage**:
- Already covered: chitin-based cuticle (Arthropoda), collagen+cuticulin cuticle (Nematoda).
- Missing and worth adding: **annelid cuticle** (Lophotrochozoa, collagen-based, non-molted), broadening `chitin-based cuticle` to **Panarthropoda** or creating an **ecdysozoan cuticle** grouping to cover Tardigrada, Onychophora, Scalidophora, Nematomorpha.
- Side note: `UBERON:0002076 cuticle of hair` is a mammalian hair-shaft layer that shares only the name with invertebrate cuticle — should be marked disjoint.

I ended the comment asking @cmungall to confirm the preferred direction before implementing, since this is a design decision (simplest option = just drop the taxon constraint; broader option = also build out the clade-specific hierarchy).

[Comment link](https://github.com/obophenotype/uberon/issues/3694#issuecomment-4317735704)
